response: 439 is a prime number. It is not divisible by any prime less than or equal to its square root (~20.95), including 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, and 19.
reasoning_content: We need to check if 439 is a prime number. Quick sanity check: Check divisibility by small primes. 439 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 4+3+9=16, not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*62=434, remainder 5, so not divisible. Check 11: 11*39=429, remainder 10, so not. Check 13: 13*33=429, remainder 10, so not. Check 17: 17*25=425, remainder 14; 17*26=442 too high. Check 19: 19*23=437, remainder 2; 19*24=456. Check 23: 23*19=437, remainder 2; 23*20=460. Check 29: 29*15=435, remainder 4; 29*16=464. Check 31: 31*14=434, remainder 5; 31*15=465. Check 37: 37*11=407, remainder 32; 37*12=444. Check 41: 41*10=410, remainder 29; 41*11=451. Check 43: 43*10=430, remainder 9; 43*11=473. Actually, we need to check up to sqrt(439) ≈ 20.95, so primes up to 19 are enough. We already checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. None divide evenly. So 439 is prime. Quick sanity: also note that 439 = 440-1, but 440 is not a perfect square, so not a difference of squares? Actually 439 = 440-1, but 440=2^3*5*11, not a square. So prime. Answer: yes, 439 is prime.
